sections.label: Section 575
sections.original: καλλίστοισι λιπαίνειν .
sections.translation: fairest streams .

sections.label: Section 576
sections.original: ἰώ ,
sections.translation: within Io ! Hear my voice , hear it , Io Bacchae , Io Bacchae !

sections.label: Section 578
sections.original: τίς ὅδε , τίς ὅδε πόθεν ὁ κέλαδος
sections.translation: Who is here , who ? From what quarter did the voice of the Joyful one summon me ?

sections.label: Section 580
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰώ , πάλιν αὐδῶ ,
sections.translation: Io ! Io ! I say again ; it is I , the child of Zeus and Semele .

sections.label: Section 582
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰὼ δέσποτα δέσποτα ,
sections.translation: Io ! Io ! Master , master ! Come now to our company , Bromius .

sections.label: Section 585
sections.original: σεῖε πέδον χθονὸς Ἔννοσι πότνια .
sections.translation: Shake the world ’ s plain , lady Earthquake !

sections.label: Section 586
sections.original: ἆ ἆ ,
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Soon the palace of Pentheus will be shaken in ruin . [*] — Dionysus is in the halls .

sections.label: Section 590
sections.original: σέβετέ νιν . — σέβομεν ὤ .
sections.translation: Revere him . — We revere him ! — Did you see these stone lintels on the pillars falling apart ? Bromius cries out in victory indoors .

sections.label: Section 594
sections.original: ἅπτε κεραύνιον αἴθοπα λαμπάδα ·
sections.translation: Light the fiery lamp of lightning !

sections.label: Section 595
sections.original: σύμφλεγε σύμφλεγε δώματα Πενθέος .
sections.translation: Burn , burn Pentheus ’ home !

sections.label: Section 596
sections.original: ἆ ἆ ,
sections.translation: Oh ! Oh ! Do you not see the the fire , do you not perceive , about the sacred tomb of Semele , the flame that Zeus ’ thunderbolt left ?

sections.label: Section 600
sections.original: δίκετε πεδόσε τρομερὰ σώματα
sections.translation: Cast on the ground your trembling bodies , Maenads , cast them down , for our lord , Zeus ’ son , is coming against this palace , turning everything upside down .

sections.label: Section 604
sections.original: βάρβαροι γυναῖκες , οὕτως ἐκπεπληγμέναι φόβῳ
sections.translation: Barbarian women , have you fallen on the ground

sections.label: Section 605
sections.original: πρὸς πέδῳ πεπτώκατʼ ; ᾔσθησθʼ , ὡς ἔοικε , Βακχίου
sections.translation: so stricken with fear ? You have , so it seems , felt Bacchus shaking the house of Pentheus . But get up and take courage , putting a stop to your trembling .

sections.label: Section 608
sections.original: ὦ φάος μέγιστον ἡμῖν εὐίου βακχεύματος ,
sections.translation: Oh greatest light for us in our joyful revelry , how happy I am to see you — I who was alone and desolate before .

sections.label: Section 610
sections.original: εἰς ἀθυμίαν ἀφίκεσθʼ , ἡνίκʼ εἰσεπεμπόμην ,
sections.translation: Did you despair when I was sent to fall into Pentheus ’ dark dungeon ?

sections.label: Section 612
sections.original: πῶς γὰρ οὔ ; τίς μοι φύλαξ ἦν , εἰ σὺ συμφορᾶς τύχοις ;
sections.translation: How not ? Who was my guardian , if you met with misfortune ? But how were you freed , having met with an impious man ?

sections.label: Section 614
sections.original: αὐτὸς ἐξέσῳσʼ ἐμαυτὸν ῥᾳδίως ἄνευ πόνου .
sections.translation: By myself I saved myself easily , without trouble .

sections.label: Section 615
sections.original: οὐδέ σου συνῆψε χεῖρε δεσμίοισιν ἐν βρόχοις ;
sections.translation: Did he not tie your hands in binding knots ?

sections.label: Section 616
sections.original: ταῦτα καὶ καθύβρισʼ αὐτόν , ὅτι με δεσμεύειν δοκῶν
sections.translation: In this too I mocked him , for , thinking to bind me , he neither touched nor handled me , but fed on hope . He found a bull by the stable where he took and shut me up , and threw shackles around its knees and hooves ,

sections.label: Section 620
sections.original: θυμὸν ἐκπνέων , ἱδρῶτα σώματος στάζων ἄπο ,
sections.translation: breathing out fury , dripping sweat from his body , gnashing his teeth in his lips . But I , being near , sitting quietly , looked on . Meanwhile , Bacchus came and shook the house and kindled a flame on his mother ’ s tomb . When Pentheus saw this , thinking that the house was burning ,

sections.label: Section 625
sections.original: ᾖσσʼ ἐκεῖσε κᾆτʼ ἐκεῖσε , δμωσὶν Ἀχελῷον φέρειν
sections.translation: he ran here and there , calling to the slaves to bring water , and every servant was at work , toiling in vain . Then he let this labor drop , as I had escaped , and snatching a dark sword rushed into the house . Then Bromius , so it seems to me — I speak my opinion —

sections.label: Section 630
sections.original: φάσμʼ ἐποίησεν κατʼ αὐλήν · ὃ δʼ ἐπὶ τοῦθʼ ὡρμημένος
sections.translation: created a phantom in the courtyard . Pentheus rushed at it headlong , stabbing at the shining air , as though slaughtering me . Besides this , Bacchus inflicted other damage on him : he knocked his house to the ground , and everything was shattered into pieces , while he saw my bitter chains . From fatigue ,

sections.label: Section 635
sections.original: διαμεθεὶς ξίφος παρεῖται · πρὸς θεὸν γὰρ ὢν ἀνὴρ
sections.translation: dropping his sword , he is exhausted . For he , a man , dared to join battle with a god . Now I have quietly left the house and come to you , with no thought of Pentheus . But I think — at any rate I hear the tramping of feet inside — he will soon come to the front of the house . What will he say after this ?

sections.label: Section 640
sections.original: ῥᾳδίως γὰρ αὐτὸν οἴσω , κἂν πνέων ἔλθῃ μέγα .
sections.translation: I shall easily bear him , even if he comes boasting greatly . For it is the part of a wise man to practice restrained good temper .

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: πέπονθα δεινά · διαπέφευγέ μʼ ὁ ξένος ,
sections.translation: I have suffered terrible things ; the stranger , who was recently constrained in bonds , has escaped me . Ah !

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ὅδʼ ἐστὶν ἁνήρ · τί τάδε ; πῶς προνώπιος
sections.translation: Here is the man . What is this ? How do you appear in front of my house , having come out ?

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: στῆσον πόδʼ , ὀργῇ δʼ ὑπόθες ἥσυχον πόδα .
sections.translation: Stop , and put a stop to your anger .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: πόθεν σὺ δεσμὰ διαφυγὼν ἔξω περᾷς ;
sections.translation: How have you escaped your chains and come outside ?

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: οὐκ εἶπον — ἢ οὐκ ἤκουσασ — ὅτι λύσει μέ τις ;
sections.translation: Did I not say — or did you not hear — that some one would deliver me ?

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: τίς ; τοὺς λόγους γὰρ ἐσφέρεις καινοὺς ἀεί .
sections.translation: Who ? You are always introducing strange explanations .

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: ὃς τὴν πολύβοτρυν ἄμπελον φύει βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: He who produces the rich-clustering vine for mortals .

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: ὠνείδισας δὴ τοῦτο Διονύσῳ καλόν .
sections.translation: You reproach Dionysus for what is his glory .

sections.label: Section 653
sections.original: κλῄειν κελεύω πάντα πύργον ἐν κύκλῳ .
sections.translation: I order you to close up all the towers around .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: τί δʼ ; οὐχ ὑπερβαίνουσι καὶ τείχη θεοί ;
sections.translation: Why ? Do gods not pass over walls too ?

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: σοφὸς σοφὸς σύ , πλὴν ἃ δεῖ σʼ εἶναι σοφόν .
sections.translation: You are wise , wise at least in all save what you should be wise in .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: ἃ δεῖ μάλιστα , ταῦτʼ ἔγωγʼ ἔφυν σοφός .
sections.translation: I was born wise in all that I should be . Enter a messenger Listen first to the words of this man , who has come from the mountain to bring you some message . I will await you , I will not try to escape .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: Πενθεῦ κρατύνων τῆσδε Θηβαίας χθονός ,
sections.translation: Pentheus , ruler of this land of Thebes , I have come from Kithairon , where the bright flakes of white snow never melt .

sections.label: Section 663
sections.original: ἥκεις δὲ ποίαν προστιθεὶς σπουδὴν λόγου ;
sections.translation: What important news do you come to bring ?

sections.label: Section 664
sections.original: βάκχας ποτνιάδας εἰσιδών , αἳ τῆσδε γῆς
sections.translation: Having seen the holy Bacchae , who

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: οἴστροισι λευκὸν κῶλον ἐξηκόντισαν ,
sections.translation: goaded to madness have darted from this land with their fair feet , I have come to tell you and the city , lord , that they are doing terrible things , beyond marvel . I wish to hear whether I should tell you in free speech the situation there or whether I should repress my report ,

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: τὸ γὰρ τάχος σου τῶν φρενῶν δέδοικʼ , ἄναξ ,
sections.translation: for I fear , lord , the quickness of your mood , your keen temper and your too imperious disposition .

sections.label: Section 672
sections.original: λέγʼ , ὡς ἀθῷος ἐξ ἐμοῦ πάντως ἔσῃ .
sections.translation: Speak , as you will have immunity from me in any case . For it is not right to be angry with the just . The more you tell me terrible things about the Bacchae ,

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: τοσῷδε μᾶλλον τὸν ὑποθέντα τὰς τέχνας
sections.translation: the more I will punish this one here who taught the women these tricks .

sections.label: Section 677
sections.original: ἀγελαῖα μὲν βοσκήματʼ ἄρτι πρὸς λέπας
sections.translation: The herds of grazing cattle were just climbing up the hill , at the time when the sun sends forth its rays , warming the earth .

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: ὁρῶ δὲ θιάσους τρεῖς γυναικείων χορῶν ,
sections.translation: I saw three companies of dancing women , one of which Autonoe led , the second your mother Agave , and the third Ino . All were asleep , their bodies relaxed , some resting their backs against pine foliage ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: αἳ δʼ ἐν δρυὸς φύλλοισι πρὸς πέδῳ κάρα
sections.translation: others laying their heads at random on the oak leaves , modestly , not as you say drunk with the goblet and the sound of the flute , hunting out Aphrodite through the woods in solitude . Your mother raised a cry ,

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: σταθεῖσα βάκχαις , ἐξ ὕπνου κινεῖν δέμας ,
sections.translation: standing up in the midst of the Bacchae , to wake their bodies from sleep , when she heard the lowing of the horned cattle . And they , casting off refreshing sleep from their eyes , sprang upright , a marvel of orderliness to behold , old , young , and still unmarried virgins .

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: καὶ πρῶτα μὲν καθεῖσαν εἰς ὤμους κόμας
sections.translation: First they let their hair loose over their shoulders , and secured their fawn-skins , as many of them as had released the fastenings of their knots , girding the dappled hides with serpents licking their jaws . And some , holding in their arms a gazelle or wild

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: ἀγρίους ἔχουσαι λευκὸν ἐδίδοσαν γάλα ,
sections.translation: wolf-pup , gave them white milk , as many as had abandoned their new-born infants and had their breasts still swollen . They put on garlands of ivy , and oak , and flowering yew . One took her thyrsos and struck it against a rock ,

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: ὅθεν δροσώδης ὕδατος ἐκπηδᾷ νοτίς ·
sections.translation: from which a dewy stream of water sprang forth . Another let her thyrsos strike the ground , and there the god sent forth a fountain of wine . All who desired the white drink scratched the earth with the tips of their fingers and obtained streams of milk ;

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: γάλακτος ἑσμοὺς εἶχον · ἐκ δὲ κισσίνων
sections.translation: and a sweet flow of honey dripped from their ivy thyrsoi ; so that , had you been present and seen this , you would have approached with prayers the god whom you now blame . We herdsmen and shepherds gathered in order to

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: κοινῶν λόγων δώσοντες ἀλλήλοις ἔριν
sections.translation: debate with one another concerning what strange and amazing things they were doing . Some one , a wanderer about the city and practised in speaking , said to us all : You who inhabit the holy plains of the mountains , do you wish to hunt

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: Πενθέως Ἀγαύην μητέρʼ ἐκ βακχευμάτων
sections.translation: Pentheus ’ mother Agave out from the Bacchic revelry and do the king a favor ? We thought he spoke well , and lay down in ambush , hiding ourselves in the foliage of bushes . They , at the appointed hour , began to wave the thyrsos in their revelries ,

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: Ἴακχον ἀθρόῳ στόματι τὸν Διὸς γόνον
sections.translation: calling on Iacchus , the son of Zeus , Bromius , with united voice . The whole mountain revelled along with them and the beasts , and nothing was unmoved by their running . 728 Agave happened to be leaping near me , and I sprang forth , wanting to snatch her ,

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: λόχμην κενώσας ἔνθʼ ἐκρυπτόμην δέμας .
sections.translation: abandoning the ambush where I had hidden myself . But she cried out : O my fleet hounds , we are hunted by these men ; but follow me ! follow armed with your thyrsoi in your hands ! We fled and escaped

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: βακχῶν σπαραγμόν , αἳ δὲ νεμομέναις χλόην
sections.translation: from being torn apart by the Bacchae , but they , with unarmed hands , sprang on the heifers browsing the grass . and you might see one rending asunder a fatted lowing calf , while others tore apart cows .

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: εἶδες δʼ ἂν ἢ πλεύρʼ ἢ δίχηλον ἔμβασιν
sections.translation: You might see ribs or cloven hooves tossed here and there ; caught in the trees they dripped , dabbled in gore . Bulls who before were fierce , and showed their fury with their horns , stumbled to the ground ,

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: μυριάσι χειρῶν ἀγόμενοι νεανίδων .
sections.translation: dragged down by countless young hands . The garment of flesh was torn apart faster then you could blink your royal eyes . And like birds raised in their course , they proceeded along the level plains , which by the streams of the Asopus
